rows.notes: Description selected from `gemini-flash`. Visual verification of the ornate script indicates 'Solicitaordinaria' is a closer match than `gpt-5-mini`'s 'Solictors ordinaries', especially for the ending of both words.

rows.description: Receipts by London Bankers. v. Messr Biddulph Cocks & Co.
rows.notes: Description is a combination. Visual verification confirms 'Receipts by London Bankers. v. Messr Biddulph' (from `gemini-flash`) and 'Cocks & Co.' (from `gpt-5-mini`). `gemini-flash` missed 'Cocks', while `gpt-5-mini` had minor inaccuracies in 'Messrs' and 'Bidulph'.
rows.section_header: Receipts by London Bankers. v. Messr Biddulph Cocks & Co.

rows.description: July 15-1793 — 3½ years Dividends on 312£.1s.10d in 3 per Cents Consolidated to July 11th 1793 —
rows.notes: Amounts are by consensus. Description from `gemini-flash` is visually more complete and accurate, including the initial date and monetary notation (312£.1s.10d). `gpt-5-mini` split this entry into two rows and had a less complete description. `gpt-5-mini`'s original row 4 was merged here.
rows.section_header: Receipts by London Bankers. v. Messr Biddulph Cocks & Co.
rows.english_description: Three and a half years' dividends on 3% consolidated government stock, credited via London bankers to 11 July 1793.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Three and a half years' dividends on 3% consolidated government stock, credited via London bankers to 11 July 1793, amounting to £14 0s 9d.

rows.description: Jan 1st 1794 one half years Dividend on 2026£.1s.10d Consd. in three per Cents to Jany 5th 1794 —
rows.notes: Amounts are by consensus. Description from `gemini-flash` is visually more complete and accurate, including the initial date, 'Consd.', and 'Jany 5th'. `gpt-5-mini` split this entry into two rows and had a less complete description with 'Consols' and 'Jany 4th'. `gpt-5-mini`'s original row 6 was merged here.
rows.section_header: Receipts by London Bankers. v. Messr Biddulph Cocks & Co.
rows.english_description: Half-year dividend on 3% consolidated government stock, credited via London bankers to 5 January 1794.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-year dividend on 3% consolidated government stock, credited via London bankers to 5 January 1794, amounting to £30 7s 9d.

rows.description: July 1st 1794 one half years Dividend on 3231£.7s.2d Consolid. 3 per Cents to July 1st 1794 —
rows.notes: Pounds and pence are by consensus. Shillings (`gemini-flash`: 9s, `gpt-5-mini`: 6s) were ambiguous visually. `gpt-5-mini`'s note provided strong reasoning, stating the choice of 6s was made because it aligned with the total amount recorded at the foot of the page. This global consistency check is highly trustworthy. The description is taken from `gemini-flash` due to better completeness and accuracy. `gpt-5-mini`'s original row 8 was merged here.
rows.section_header: Receipts by London Bankers. v. Messr Biddulph Cocks & Co.
rows.english_description: Half-year dividend on 3% consolidated government stock, credited via London bankers to 1 July 1794.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-year dividend on 3% consolidated government stock, credited via London bankers to 1 July 1794, amounting to £48 6s 4d.

rows.description: Oct 1st 1794 two half years Dividends on 546£ to Octr 10th 1794 5 per Cents payable at LLD & Miss ½ years —
rows.notes: Pounds and pence are by consensus. Shillings (`gemini-flash`: 10s, `gpt-5-mini`: 16s) were ambiguous visually, though `gemini-flash`'s 10s initially appeared more likely. However, `gpt-5-mini`'s value of 16s, when combined with its 6s for the previous row, is necessary to achieve the grand total of £164 11s 6d written at the foot of the page. This strong consistency with the ledger's own sum overrides individual visual ambiguity. Description from `gemini-flash` is more accurate for the date 'Octr 10th' and 'LLD'.
rows.section_header: Receipts by London Bankers. v. Messr Biddulph Cocks & Co.
rows.english_description: Two half-years' dividends (i.e. one year's dividends) on 5% government stock, credited via London bankers to 10 October 1794.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Two half-years' dividends on 5% government stock, credited via London bankers to 10 October 1794, amounting to £21 16s 8d.

rows.notes: Amounts are by consensus. Description from `gpt-5-mini` ('East India Annuity') is visually more accurate than `gemini-flash`'s 'Eachir. Annuity'.
rows.section_header: Receipts by London Bankers. v. Messr Biddulph Cocks & Co.
rows.english_description: One year's annuity from the East India Company, credited via London bankers.
rows.english_description_with_amount: One year's annuity from the East India Company, credited via London bankers, amounting to £50 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Amounts are by consensus and directly match the visible total. Description from `gemini-flash` is more accurate, specifically 'Messr' (singular), 'Biddulph' (double 'd'), and includes the final period.
